description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and sharply defined, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The stone has a dark, almost black coloration with subtle gray marbling, suggesting it is made from a dense, polished material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The block is solid and heavy, likely used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional features beyond the grooved surface.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed, striped appearance. The block is solid, heavy, and made from polished stone, suitable as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block with horizontal grooves visible across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the vertical grooves, with the smooth side face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the block's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the smooth, unmarked side face.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a pedestrian barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surface appears clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
